LLD learning path

Extensibility and common LLD patterns

Use patterns as decision tools, not cargo cults, so your design stays open to change without drowning in abstraction.

Module progress 0%

0 of 3 lessons complete

Next up Lesson 1: Strategy, factory, and builder patterns in interviews

Recognize when these patterns clarify construction or runtime variation, and when plain code is simpler.

Why this module matters Design responsibilities cleanly before patterns start to stack up.

Move through the lessons in order so each object-model decision has context before follow-up requirements and machine-coding trade-offs appear.

Study target 3 lessons left

Aim to finish one lesson, then explain its core trade-off out loud before moving on.

Low-level design interview prep 3 lessons 0/3 complete Follow in order for best results Back to LLD roadmap Start module View lesson runway

Learning rhythm

Study this like a sequenced course, not a reference dump

  1. Preview the runway. Skim every lesson title first so you can see the progression before you dive in.
  2. Do one lesson deeply. Read the prompt shape, object boundaries, and extension seams before coding anything.
  3. Say it back aloud. Explain the idea in interviewer language before you mark it complete.
  4. Only then advance. Move to the next lesson when you can defend the previous one without the notes open.
LLD sequence Designed for interview recall

Outcomes

What you should walk away with

  • Apply core patterns only when they solve a real axis of change
  • Use dependency inversion to isolate volatile collaborators
  • Keep pattern-heavy answers readable under interview time pressure

Practice flow

How to study each lesson

  • Read the summary and explain the core object or workflow decision in your own words.
  • Sketch the key classes, responsibilities, and extension seams before you code.
  • Answer the interview prompts aloud before marking the lesson complete.

Mastery check

How you know it is sticking

  • Clear assumptions, invariants, and object boundaries.
  • Justified abstraction choices instead of pattern dumping.
  • Awareness of follow-ups such as testing, concurrency, and extensibility.

Lesson runway

Follow the sequence from first principles to fluency

This is the core pattern borrowed from stronger learning products: show the path, make the next step obvious, and keep outcomes visible while the learner moves.

Strategy, factory, and builder patterns in interviews

Recognize when these patterns clarify construction or runtime variation, and when plain code is simpler.

25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Lesson 2 Open
Observer, dependency inversion, and event-style decoupling

Decouple workflows from side effects so new subscribers or integrations can be added without rewriting core business logic.

25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Lesson 3 Open
Repositories, caching, and persistence seams

Keep storage details swappable enough for testing and iteration without pretending persistence does not shape the domain.

25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections

Lesson 1

Strategy, factory, and builder patterns in interviews

Open

Recognize when these patterns clarify construction or runtime variation, and when plain code is simpler.

Why it matters

These are common interview patterns because they solve real change vectors, but overusing them signals memorization rather than design judgment.

What you will cover

Use strategy for runtime variationUse factories for creation complexityUse builders for readable setup

Good interview signal

  • Tie each pattern to a real change driver.
  • Keep pattern interfaces narrow and intention-revealing.
25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson

Lesson 2

Observer, dependency inversion, and event-style decoupling

Open

Decouple workflows from side effects so new subscribers or integrations can be added without rewriting core business logic.

Why it matters

Many LLD follow-ups ask how to add notifications, analytics, or plugins. This is where explicit event seams and dependency inversion become practical design tools.

What you will cover

Separate the core action from side effectsUse dependency inversion thoughtfullyDiscuss operational trade-offs

Good interview signal

  • Keep core rules independent from side effects.
  • Use abstractions to isolate infrastructure choices.
25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson

Lesson 3

Repositories, caching, and persistence seams

Open

Keep storage details swappable enough for testing and iteration without pretending persistence does not shape the domain.

Why it matters

Low-level designs improve when persistence concerns are explicit but do not leak everywhere. This is especially important in interview prompts that may later add caching or new storage backends.

What you will cover

Define the persistence contractAdd caching without muddying ownershipProtect testability and migration paths

Good interview signal

  • Model persistence APIs around domain use cases.
  • State who owns caching and freshness guarantees.
25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson
